articles.headline: Here’s How Much Wider The Airbus A350 Cabin Is Compared To The Boeing 787 Dreamliner
articles.blurb: The Airbus A350 cabin is 5 inches wider than the Boeing 787—here’s how that small difference transforms seat comfort and long-haul travel.
articles.takeaway: The A350's 5-inch cabin width advantage enables three-abreast business class configurations versus two-abreast on the 787, directly impacting airline revenue per seat on premium routes.

articles.headline: Washington Repeals Aircraft Tax Before It Takes Effect
articles.blurb: Measure enacted in 2025 is replaced with fuel tax and fee increases.
articles.takeaway: Washington's shift from aircraft-specific taxation to fuel and fee structures may create more competitive pricing for in-state operators while redistributing cost burden across broader aviation activities.

articles.headline: Global Combat Air Programme Awards First Contract with Edgewing
articles.blurb: The first joint international contract has been awarded for the Global Combat Air Programme (GCAP), focusing on key design and engineering activities. The GCAP International Government Organisation (GIGO), which represents the governments of the UK, Italy and Japan, awarded a £686 million contract...
articles.takeaway: The £686 million initial GCAP contract signals that tripartite defense cooperation between UK, Italy, and Japan is moving from planning to execution on a next-generation fighter program.

articles.headline: The Evolution of Aviation SMS
articles.blurb: From the Wright brothers to the 2024 FAA rule — how aviation safety evolved from reactive investigation to proactive risk management.
articles.takeaway: Modern aviation SMS shifts the industry paradigm from investigating accidents after they occur to systematically identifying and mitigating hazards before they cause incidents.

articles.headline: How the Iran War Is Rewiring Avionics

articles.headline: Silicon Over Steel: The Avionics Reset That Stole the Show at ISTAT Americas 2026

articles.takeaway: Semiconductor-based avionics are replacing traditional metal-core systems, signaling a fundamental architecture shift that will reshape maintenance, certification, and lifecycle economics across commercial fleets.

articles.headline: Climate Change Is Rewriting the Rules of Turbulence—and Cockpits Are Playing Catch-Up
articles.blurb: Global Avionics Round-Up from Aircraft Value News (AVN) For decades, turbulence was treated as a known nuisance—unpredictable, occasionally dangerous, but broadly manageable within the design assumptions of modern aircraft. Engineers […]...
articles.takeaway: Climate-driven turbulence changes are exceeding historical design parameters, forcing avionics and aircraft systems to evolve beyond decades-old assumptions.

articles.headline: Safety Culture in Aviation SMS
articles.blurb: How organizational culture shapes safety outcomes and what it takes to build a reporting culture that supports SMS.
articles.takeaway: SMS effectiveness depends less on process documentation than on psychological safety enabling frontline employees to report hazards without fear of retaliation.

articles.headline: Army’s SkyFoundry Plan To Build Own Cheap Drones Has ‘Evolved,’ Official Notes Commercial Involvement

articles.headline: From the Flight Deck – Eastern Iowa Airport, IA (CID)

articles.takeaway: Air France-KLM's pre-emptive stake in TAP positions it to shape European consolidation before competitors can act, potentially reshaping Iberian aviation networks.

articles.headline: Qatar Airways Suspends Flights To 64 Destinations [Full List & Map]

articles.takeaway: Regional geopolitical tensions are creating operational ripple effects across Gulf carriers, forcing Qatar Airways to rationalize its network during a critical recovery period.

articles.headline: Moog Technology Successfully Steers Artemis II Launch

articles.headline: RNO Mustang 9 Departure

articles.headline: Gone After Just 1 Year: Virgin Atlantic Axes This Long-Haul Route From London

articles.takeaway: Virgin Atlantic's rapid withdrawal from Riyadh signals capacity reallocation challenges as carriers reassess long-haul profitability in competitive Gulf markets amid shifting demand patterns.

articles.headline: The US Air Force Pilots With The Highest Salaries In 2026

articles.takeaway: Air Force pilot compensation structures now include performance-based bonuses and retention incentives, reflecting increased competition for experienced aviators amid pilot shortage pressures.

articles.headline: Carmel Ortiz on the evolving landscape of satellite communications

articles.takeaway: MEO satellite constellations are emerging as a competitive alternative to LEO networks, offering lower latency than GEO while reducing the scale required compared to LEO deployments.

articles.headline: Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization
articles.blurb: Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.
articles.takeaway: Operational optimization across the entire aviation system can simultaneously address capacity constraints and environmental concerns without requiring complete fleet replacement.
keywords: aviation; optimization; operations research; sustainability; environmental impacts

articles.headline: FAA Clears Key Step for ZeroAvia Electric Engine
articles.blurb: Publication of special conditions marks important certification step for electric regional propulsion system.
articles.takeaway: ZeroAvia's special conditions approval signals FAA readiness to establish certification pathways for electric regional aircraft, potentially accelerating decarbonization of 19-seat to 75-seat market segments.

articles.headline: 5 Reasons Why The Boeing 777X Needs Folding Wingtips
articles.blurb: The Boeing 777X's folding wingtips are a critical solution to a fundamental design conflict between airline efficiency and airport infrastructure.
articles.takeaway: Folding wingtips solve a critical constraint: airlines demand larger, more efficient aircraft but most airports cannot accommodate the 777X's 235-foot wingspan without costly infrastructure upgrades.

articles.blurb: City officials cite costs and redevelopment potential, while others raise concerns over process and economic impact.

articles.headline: Why Seat Width & Pitch Vary So Much Between US Airlines
articles.blurb: No two airlines in the US are really quite the same, especially in terms of seats. What makes each airline so drastically different?
articles.takeaway: Airlines optimize seat configurations independently without federal standards, allowing carriers to extract maximum revenue from identical aircraft types through varying density strategies.

articles.headline: How Rolls-Royce Engines Differ From General Electric Engines
articles.blurb: Both engine manufacturers power some of the world's largest commercial jets.
articles.takeaway: Rolls-Royce and GE engines differ primarily in bypass ratio design philosophy and maintenance intervals, affecting airline operating costs and fleet commonality strategies.

articles.headline: Phantom Space buys thermal specialist to support orbital data center push

articles.takeaway: Vertical integration into thermal management addresses a critical engineering bottleneck for orbital data centers operating in extreme temperature environments.
